behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: This failure is primarily about module handoff (ingestion/parsing/correlation/grouping) across service-originated records, not a single-function algorithm. No behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess or AI output, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process pipeline test that feeds mixed JSON logs from multiple mocked microservice sources, then assert all records sharing a trace ID end up in exactly one correlated group.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: Although timestamp ordering logic may be unit-testable, this behavior is about end-to-end timeline construction across records from different services with skew normalization rules, which is a cross-module interaction. No explicit real external contract/round-trip side effect or AI dependence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed correlated multi-service logs with controlled ±500ms skew through the timeline builder stack and assert final timeline order matches expected causal/normalized order.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Detection requires interaction between baseline computation, percentile aggregation, thresholding, and report flagging modules. Behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess contracts, and there is no model-output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the anomaly pipeline with synthetic latency distributions that establish a baseline then exceed 3x at p99; assert the generated anomaly report contains the spike flag and relevant metric values.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: This is a resilience and state-propagation behavior across parser, pipeline control flow, and reporting/metrics modules. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess behavior or AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full in-process ingestion pipeline with a mix of valid and malformed JSON entries; assert processing continues, valid entries are preserved, and skipped-entry count matches malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real runtime characteristics (timing, scheduling, buffering/backpressure) and proving externally visible effects under load (no drops, no lag) via round-trip observation. Mocked/in-process-only components can hide real throughput failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the tool as a real process under a load generator at 10,000 entries/sec, then verify via output/readback metrics that processed count equals sent count and end-to-end lag stays within defined bounds during and after the burst.
